resumeLength: One page for early-career roles; keep academic projects concise and decision-focused.

formattingRules.fontRecommendation: Clean business-style font and conventional headings.
formattingRules.bulletStyle: Bullets should read like compact business evidence, not class descriptions.

writingRules.bulletFormula: Action + business/economic question + method/data + deliverable/recommendation + implication or stakeholder value
quantificationRules: Prefer concrete scope: sample size, stakeholder count, case volume, event size, model accuracy, project duration, budget, risk, or response time when supported by source facts.
When exact numbers are absent, use bounded evidence such as tool, method, deliverable, audience, or process scope; never invent metrics.
Use metrics differently by domain: technical performance for engineering, compliance/accuracy for audit, writing/research output for law and policy, service reliability for hospitality.

exampleBullets.strong: Modeled demand scenarios using public market data and presented pricing implications in a team research report.
exampleBullets.reason: Turns coursework into question, method, output, and business implication.

exampleBullets.strong: Benchmarked competitor offerings and summarized positioning gaps to support a market-entry recommendation.
exampleBullets.reason: Adds analytical method and decision context.

scoringCriteria.description: Conventional headings, readable layout, aligned dates, consistent spacing, and parser-friendly text.
Prominent sections and bullets match the target role and market context.
Bullets show action, method, scope, and outcome without unsupported claims.
Specific, concise, role-native language with no inflated AI phrasing.
Structure and density match the selected role, country, and CV/resume style.

promptSnippets.title: Economics and Business Management Resume routing rule
promptSnippets.content: When the target role matches Economics / Business Management, use Economics and Business Management Resume as the primary industry route and adopt this formula: Action + business/economic question + method/data + deliverable/recommendation + implication or stakeholder value.

promptSnippets.title: Economics and Business Management Resume language guardrail
promptSnippets.content: Preserve domain-native evidence for Economics / Business Management; do not convert the profile into a generic tech, finance, or consulting resume unless the user target explicitly requires it.

sourceReferences.note: 56 newly added CV/resume samples from docs/sample-resume-knowledge; abstracted into rules only, without copying resume text.
